title: Mihai Zamfir (footballer)

infobox.value: (born 22 January 1955) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former football [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") and [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)").[[3]](#cite_note-manager-3)

**Mihai Zamfir** (born 22 January 1955) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former football [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") and [manager](/wiki/Manager_(association_football) "Manager (association football)").[[3]](#cite_note-manager-3)

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mihai_Zamfir&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Zamfir was born on 22 January 1955 in [Moșoaia](/wiki/Mo%C8%99oaia "Moșoaia"), Romania and began playing junior-level football in 1971 at [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) He made his [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") debut on 30 September 1973 under coach [Ștefan Coidum](/wiki/%C8%98tefan_Coidum "Ștefan Coidum") in a 2–1 home victory against [Sportul Studențesc București](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the [1978–79 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_UEFA_Cup "1978–79 UEFA Cup") edition he helped the team eliminate [Panathinaikos](/wiki/Panathinaikos_F.C. "Panathinaikos F.C.") in the first round with a 5–1 aggregate victory.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[5]](#cite_note-WF-5) In the following round they met [Valencia](/wiki/Valencia_CF "Valencia CF") led by [Mario Kempes](/wiki/Mario_Kempes "Mario Kempes"), earning a 2–1 win in the first leg, but they lost the second one with 5–2, thus the campaign ended.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[5]](#cite_note-WF-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) In the [same season](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A") he helped Argeș win the title, being used by coach [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") in 33 games in which he scored two goals.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[7]](#cite_note-7) In the following season they got past [AEK Athens](/wiki/AEK_Athens_F.C. "AEK Athens F.C.") in the first round of the [1979–80 European Cup](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_European_Cup "1979–80 European Cup"), the team being eliminated in the following one by title holders and eventual winners, [Nottingham Forest](/wiki/Nottingham_Forest_F.C. "Nottingham Forest F.C."), Zamfir receiving a red card for repeated fouls in the 80th minute of the first leg.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 1984 he joined [Olt Scornicești](/wiki/FC_Olt_Scornice%C8%99ti "FC Olt Scornicești") where he made his last Divizia A appearance on 17 December 1987 in a 0–0 draw against [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), totaling 365 matches with 11 goals in the competition and 12 games in European competitions.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1) Zamfir ended his career in 1989, after playing in [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") for [Inter Sibiu](/wiki/FC_Inter_Sibiu "FC Inter Sibiu") and [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești"), helping the first earn promotion to the first league.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mihai_Zamfir&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Zamfir played 10 games for [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), making his debut when coach [Ștefan Kovács](/wiki/%C8%98tefan_Kov%C3%A1cs "Ștefan Kovács") sent him in the 24th minute to replace [Florin Cheran](/wiki/Florin_Cheran "Florin Cheran") in a 2–0 win over [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria_national_football_team "Bulgaria national football team") in the [1977–80 Balkan Cup](/wiki/1977%E2%80%9380_Balkan_Cup "1977–80 Balkan Cup").[[9]](#cite_note-EU-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) He also played in the second leg against Bulgaria which ended in a 1–1 draw.[[9]](#cite_note-EU-9)[[11]](#cite_note-11) Subsequently, he made three appearances in the [Euro 1980 qualifiers](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_1980_qualifying_Group_3 "UEFA Euro 1980 qualifying Group 3").[[9]](#cite_note-EU-9) Zamfir's last appearance occurred on 8 April 1981 in a 2–1 away loss in a friendly against [Israel](/wiki/Israel_national_football_team "Israel national football team").[[9]](#cite_note-EU-9)

## Managerial career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mihai_Zamfir&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Managerial career")]

Zamfir coached several clubs in Romanian football, starting with [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești"), then working for [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești"), Chimica Târnăveni, [Politehnica Timișoara](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_Timi%C8%99oara "FC Politehnica Timișoara"), [Cimentul Fieni](/wiki/AS_Cimentul_Fieni "AS Cimentul Fieni"), [Unirea Alba Iulia](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Alba_Iulia "FC Unirea Alba Iulia") and Girom Albota.[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)[[3]](#cite_note-manager-3) He also worked as an assistant for [Nicolae Dobrin](/wiki/Nicolae_Dobrin "Nicolae Dobrin") and [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") at Argeș Pitești, and for Cornel Iliescu at [Dacia Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni").[[3]](#cite_note-manager-3)[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2) Zamfir coached abroad at the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") club Al Sabah.[[3]](#cite_note-manager-3)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mihai_Zamfir&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Personal life")]

His wife, Dana, was a [handball](/wiki/Handball "Handball") player and his daughter Cristina played [volleyball](/wiki/Volleyball "Volleyball").[[2]](#cite_note-arges-2)[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Mihai_Zamfir&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Argeș Pitești**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1978–79](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

**Inter Sibiu**

* [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): [1987–88](/wiki/1987%E2%80%9388_Divizia_B "1987–88 Divizia B")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
